raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft requires assessing whether the contribution claim (multi-agent loop recovers genuine errors rather than surface paraphrases) and the gap statement are clearly and correctly framed; the current framing is asserted but not anchored to a stated limitation in prior work.","R1.2":"The convergence argument in paragraph 3 — that shared underlying parameters imply the Editor's output will be accepted within a few rounds — is logically incomplete; the inference does not follow from the premise alone, and improving the draft requires catching and repairing such premise-to-conclusion gaps.","R1.3":"The concluding paragraph makes strong generalisation claims ('general-purpose framework', 'any LLM-based translation system') that outpace evidence drawn from one base model across three language pairs; improvement must assess whether claim strength is calibrated to the reported results.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing for any 'improve this draft' delegation — sentence-level clarity, concision, and paragraph cohesion are the minimum expected output regardless of how deep the revision goes."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvements address only surface writing quality (clarity, concision, phrasing), or also the logical and argumentative substance — such as the convergence justification in paragraph 3 and the strength of the concluding generalisation?"}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft requires assessing whether the contribution claim (multi-agent loop recovers genuine errors rather than surface paraphrases) and the gap statement are clearly and correctly framed; the current framing is asserted but not anchored to a stated limitation in prior work.
rationale.R1.2: The convergence argument in paragraph 3 — that shared underlying parameters imply the Editor's output will be accepted within a few rounds — is logically incomplete; the inference does not follow from the premise alone, and improving the draft requires catching and repairing such premise-to-conclusion gaps.
rationale.R1.3: The concluding paragraph makes strong generalisation claims ('general-purpose framework', 'any LLM-based translation system') that outpace evidence drawn from one base model across three language pairs; improvement must assess whether claim strength is calibrated to the reported results.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing for any 'improve this draft' delegation — sentence-level clarity, concision, and paragraph cohesion are the minimum expected output regardless of how deep the revision goes.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements address only surface writing quality (clarity, concision, phrasing), or also the logical and argumentative substance — such as the convergence justification in paragraph 3 and the strength of the concluding generalisation?
